message:
mt-wasm fix: per-worker shadow stacks resolve the parallel race

Root cause found: every WebAssembly.Instance starts with
__stack_pointer initialised to the same default value (1 MB into
linear memory, set by wasm-ld's --shared-memory pass). The
__stack_pointer global is per-instance, but the *stack memory*
those pointers reference lives in the shared linear memory. With
N workers all defaulting to the same pointer, every concurrent
kernel call pushed locals to the same address and clobbered each
other's stack frames — exactly what bisection narrowed to as
'matmul down + scatter combined trigger the race', because that
chain is what spilled the most locals to the shared shadow stack.

Fix:
- Build mt-wasm with `--export=__stack_pointer` so the host can set
  it per worker.
- WasmBackend.warmup allocates 256 KB of shadow-stack space per
  worker (in shared linear memory, after the heap_mark) and hands
  each worker its dedicated stack-top.
- Worker init message carries `stackTop`; before the first task,
  the worker writes `wasm.__stack_pointer.value = stackTop`. Stack
  grows downward from there, so each worker's frames stay inside
  its own region.

Verified:
- ST vs MT(N=4): logit maxDiff 1.56e-2 (just fp16 rounding noise
  from per-worker cast), argmax bit-exact.
- Run-to-run determinism on N=4: maxDiff 0.00 across many runs.
- Node bench (T=32): single=231.7ms, mt(2)=161.0ms (1.44x),
  mt(4)=113.4ms (2.04x).
- Browser smoke + webgpu-smoke + public-api still pass — Playwright
  test server doesn't ship COOP/COEP, so browser falls back to
  single-thread, which is also exercised correctly.

Default flipped to multiThread:"auto" — Node and cross-origin-
isolated browsers get the fast path automatically; everywhere else
falls back to single-thread without ceremony.

The scatter_add_weighted_f32_scalar export added during bisection
to disambiguate SIMD-vs-stack hypotheses is left in place; the
SIMD variant is what we use, the scalar variant stays for
debugging future regressions.

Removes the bisect debug logs in mt-expert.ts.

files.patch:
@@ -38,7 +38,7 @@
     "build": "npm run build:zig && npm run build:opt && node scripts/inline-wasm.mjs && bun build src/js/index.ts --outdir dist --target browser --format esm --sourcemap=external && bun x tsc --emitDeclarationOnly",
     "build:zig": "npm run build:zig-st && npm run build:zig-mt",
     "build:zig-st": "mise exec -- zig build-exe -target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd -O ReleaseFast -fstrip -flto src/zig/wasm_exports.zig -cflags -O3 -mrelaxed-simd -flto -- src/c/fma.c --name pii -fno-entry -rdynamic && mv pii.wasm dist/",
-    "build:zig-mt": "mise exec -- zig build-exe -target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd+atomics+bulk_memory -O ReleaseFast -fstrip -flto src/zig/wasm_exports.zig -cflags -O3 -mrelaxed-simd -flto -- src/c/fma.c --shared-memory --import-memory --initial-memory=4194304 --max-memory=2147483648 --name pii-mt -fno-entry -rdynamic && mv pii-mt.wasm dist/",
+    "build:zig-mt": "mise exec -- zig build-exe -target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd+atomics+bulk_memory -O ReleaseFast -fstrip -flto src/zig/wasm_exports.zig -cflags -O3 -mrelaxed-simd -flto -- src/c/fma.c --shared-memory --import-memory --initial-memory=4194304 --max-memory=2147483648 --export=__stack_pointer --name pii-mt -fno-entry -rdynamic && mv pii-mt.wasm dist/",
     "build:opt": "npm run build:opt-st && npm run build:opt-mt",
     "build:opt-st": "wasm-opt -O3 --strip-producers --strip-target-features dist/pii.wasm -o dist/pii.wasm --enable-simd --enable-bulk-memory --enable-nontrapping-float-to-int --enable-sign-ext --enable-mutable-globals --enable-relaxed-simd --inlining-optimizing --always-inline-max-function-size 256 --flexible-inline-max-function-size 512 --inline-functions-with-loops",
     "build:opt-mt": "wasm-opt -O3 --strip-producers --strip-target-features dist/pii-mt.wasm -o dist/pii-mt.wasm --enable-simd --enable-bulk-memory --enable-nontrapping-float-to-int --enable-sign-ext --enable-mutable-globals --enable-relaxed-simd --enable-threads --inlining-optimizing --always-inline-max-function-size 256 --flexible-inline-max-function-size 512 --inline-functions-with-loops",
